title: Myriopteris tomentosa
intro: Myriopteris tomentosa, also known as the hairy maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to North America, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds with a hairy rachis. Traditional healers have long valued Myriopteris tomentosa for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. In herbal medicine, Myriopteris tomentosa is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to create a calming and restorative drink, or it is used in tinctures and capsules to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Myriopteris tomentosa is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. This plant is also known to be part of the genus Myriopteris, which comprises various species of ferns. The classification of Myriopteris tomentosa further includes its placement within the order Polypodiales and division Polypodiophyta.
